creator: citycat4@ngs.ru
text:
I have recently installed internal card reader with usual 4 separate card
readers, one USB port, one firewire port (not used, m/b hasn't any) and
one E-SATA port. When any card reader or USB port are empty, kernel loading
stops after 

Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: usb1: <UHCI (generic) USB controller> on uhci1
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: usb1: USB revision 1.0
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhub1: <Intel UHCI root hub, class 9/0, rev 1.00/1.00, addr 1> on usb1
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhub1: 2 ports with 2 removable, self powered
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhci2: <UHCI (generic) USB controller> port 0xd000-0xd01f irq 18 at device 26.2 on pci0
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhci2: [GIANT-LOCKED]
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhci2: [ITHREAD]
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: usb2: <UHCI (generic) USB controller> on uhci2
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: usb2: USB revision 1.0
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhub2: <Intel UHCI root hub, class 9/0, rev 1.00/1.00, addr 1> on usb2
Aug 25 22:59:38 citycat kernel: uhub2: 2 ports with 2 removable, self powered

and system freezes forever.

When I boot with any inserted card in reader or inserted USB Flash - boot OK.

M/b Gigabyte GA-P35-S3, Intel P35-based, BIOS latest

Fix: 

Insert any card in reader or USB Flash before boot
How-To-Repeat: Install internal card reader in box with similar hardware and try to boot.
